title: Server Cold Swap / Component Replacement
description: Power-down replacement of a failed server or non-hot-swap component.
safety_notes: Confirm an approved maintenance window and that the workload is drained/failed over.
Servers have dual PSUs — remove BOTH feeds to de-energize; LOTO as required.
Wear an ESD strap when handling DIMMs, cards, or drives.

steps.title: Confirm window and failover
steps.instruction: Confirm the maintenance window is approved and the workload has been failed over or drained. Is it safe to power down this server?
steps.safety_note: Do not proceed without an approved window.

branches.condition: Not approved or still serving load

steps.title: Stop — not safe
steps.instruction: Do not power down. Coordinate failover/drain and an approved window first, then restart this procedure.

steps.title: Positively identify the unit
steps.instruction: Use iDRAC/iLO identify-LED and the service tag/serial to confirm you have the correct chassis. Photograph the service tag. Confirmed?

steps.title: Graceful power down and de-energize
steps.instruction: Gracefully shut down the OS, then power off. Remove BOTH power feeds and confirm the chassis is de-energized.
steps.expected_input: powered off and both feeds removed
steps.safety_note: Both PSU feeds must be removed — one PSU does not de-energize the chassis.

steps.title: Replace the component
steps.instruction: With an ESD strap, replace the failed component (label cables/drive bays first). Photograph before/after for the record.

steps.title: Power up and verify
steps.instruction: Reconnect both feeds, power on, and watch POST + iDRAC/iLO health. Is system health green and the workload restored?

steps.title: Complete
steps.instruction: Server back in service and healthy. Update inventory with the replaced part and serials.

steps.title: Escalate — post-swap fault
steps.instruction: Health fault persists after the swap. Capture iDRAC/iLO logs and escalate to senior support before returning to production.
